items.title: Overhaul could affect how more than 1 million Australians access JobSeeker

children.content: Jobseeker services will shift away from a one-size-fits-all approach to a new tiered model, to be unveiled by the government later today.

articles.title: Employment minister unveils sweeping changes to unemployment system

children.content: Employment Minister Amanda Rishworth details the "once-in-a-generation" changes the federal government is making to Australia's unemployment system. Follow live.

image.altText: Rishworth speaks at the NPC lectern

articles.title: Iran war derailed talks to claw back billions in tax credits paid to miners

children.content: Billions in tax credits are paid to coal miners every year. Ministers discussed clawing back some of that money in the May federal budget, but the Iran war has pushed out those talks.

image.altText: A female mine worker walks past a mine dump truck at a coal mine in central Queensland.

gallery.altText: A blurry image of people queueing to enter a Centrelink office to apply for unemployment benefits.

thumbnail.altText: A blurry image of people queueing to enter a Centrelink office to apply for unemployment benefits.

items.title: ABC's Justin Stevens resigns as director of news

children.content: Justin Stevens, the ABC's director of news, has announced his resignation after 19 years with the organisation.

image.altText: Headshot of Justin Stevens

items.title: Inquiry told giving Jewish security groups greater power 'problematic'

children.content: NSW Police Deputy Commissioner David Hudson has given evidence at the Royal Commission on Antisemitism and Social Cohesion.

image.altText: Officer David Hudson in a blue uniform entering the royal commission building

items.title: Media leaks ahead of Ben Roberts-Smith arrest referred to corruption commission

children.content: News crews were at Sydney Airport as a plane carrying Ben Roberts-Smith landed, and captured images of his arrest from inside the terminal.

image.altText: Ben Roberts-Smith, face blurred, exits plane onto Sydney Airport tarmac flanked by officers and men in suits.

items.title: Russia looks for new soldiers in Transnistria as war casualty numbers grow

children.content: The Kremlin is trying to recruit more soldiers for its war in Ukraine but an analyst says at the rate of casualties, "Russia cannot replace recruits fast enough to keep the 'meat grinder' fed."

image.altText: Russian President Vladimir Putin  reaches his hand across a table

items.title: NASA has revealed its plan to live on the Moon. Here's how it would work

children.content: The US space agency has outlined plans for a sprawling lunar outpost involving rovers, hopping drones and resource-extraction technology as part of a renewed race to the Moon.

gallery.altText: An artist's rendering showing astronauts, habitats, rovers, power systems and cargo operations at the Moon Base.
Artist’s rendering depicting lunar surface operations at a future base in the lunar South Pole.
Artist’s rendering of the lunar South Pole region

thumbnail.altText: An artist's rendering showing astronauts, habitats, rovers, power systems and cargo operations at the Moon Base.
Artist’s rendering depicting lunar surface operations at a future base in the lunar South Pole.
Artist’s rendering of the lunar South Pole region

items.title: Headline inflation eases to 4.2 per cent in April as fuel prices fall

children.content: Headline inflation decelerated in April, with consumer prices increasing at an annual pace of 4.2 per cent, down from 4.6 per cent in March.

image.altText: photo of a hand with empty cup of coffee and cash on a wooden table

items.title: Odds of June rate hike close to zero after inflation fall

children.content: Today's inflation announcement reveals rates have eased to below what was expected, suggesting the Reserve Bank may not raise cash rates next month. Follow live.

image.altText: Business people walk past a shiny black sign that reads "Reserve Bank of Australia".

items.title: Australian kids are gambling long before they turn 18

children.content: From footy tips to Taylor Swift bets, teenagers are being lured into gambling. Almost 34 per cent of Australian teenagers gamble and everyone from teachers, experts, to the young people themselves are sounding the alarm.

gallery.altText: A boy leaning against a brick wall looks down at his phone.
Boy wearing a hoodie looks at his phone.
A boy with bruised knuckles sits and looks at his mobile phone.

thumbnail.altText: A boy leaning against a brick wall looks down at his phone.
Boy wearing a hoodie looks at his phone.
A boy with bruised knuckles sits and looks at his mobile phone.

items.title: Iran calls latest US strikes 'bad faith' sign as internet slowly restored

children.content: Iran has denounced US strikes in the country's south as a sign of "bad faith and unreliability" as negotiations toward a possible deal to end the war continue.

image.altText: Smoke billows across a city.

items.title: The BHP files reveal a big problem facing the mining giant

children.content: For a company that prides itself on digging out the metals for the world's clean energy future, saying nothing about its own clean energy future is not an option.

image.altText: Mining infrastructure and machinery in pits of red dirt.

items.title: More than a million worshippers brave searing heat for annual Hajj pilgrimage

children.content: Thousands of Muslim worshippers converge on the rocky hills and plains of Saudi Arabia's Mount Arafat as the annual Islamic pilgrimage, the Hajj, reached its peak.

gallery.altText: men pray against a rising sun
men in white robes stand on top of a hill against a rising sun horizon

thumbnail.altText: men pray against a rising sun
men in white robes stand on top of a hill against a rising sun horizon

items.title: Woman forced out of home as mould grows after insurer repairs roof

children.content: Lily lodged an insurance claim with her provider GIO Suncorp after a truck ran through her street's power lines. Nearly a decade later, she has been forced out of her home.

image.altText: Lily looks at the camera in her garden wearing a red shirt

items.title: Police search for missing swimmer in WA's South West

children.content: Police say the woman entered the water at Koombana Bay just after 9:20am and has not been seen since

image.altText: Police standing at the shoreline of Koombana Bay

items.title: Why Blak Douglas won't enter the Archibald Prize again

children.content: The acclaimed Dunghutti artist is using his latest exhibition to challenge perceptions of Aboriginal art and the systemic issues creatives face in Australia.

gallery.altText: Artist Blak Douglas, born Adam Douglas Hill, has opened a new exhibition at NSW Parliament House entitled 'Home Flown'.

thumbnail.altText: Artist Blak Douglas, born Adam Douglas Hill, has opened a new exhibition at NSW Parliament House entitled 'Home Flown'.

items.title: Cuts of meat that can save you money and how to cook with them

children.content: If you are looking to trim your household food budget, lower-cost cuts of meat could be a good place to start. Here are some tips on what to look for and how to cook them.

image.altText: Two women looking at a packet of pre-packaged meat at a supermarket

items.title: More or Less quiz: We need you to rank five State of Origin greats

children.content: If you think you know your stuff when it comes to State of Origin, here's your chance to prove it.

items.title: 'About bloody time': BOM selects new NT radar site 11 years after closure

children.content: BOM says it has decided to install a new weather radar in Tennant Creek, in the same place as the previous radar.

items.title: 'Inadequate' jail sentence for sex worker killer increased after appeal

children.content: Xiaozheng Lin will spend more time in prison for killing two sex workers in 2022, following an appeal by the prosecutor.

image.altText: Xiaozheng Lin stands against a grey backdrop in a police mug shot.

items.title: Heavy rain on Gus Lamont's family station prompts police to renew search

children.content: Eight months since Gus Lamont was reported missing, police have again returned to search Oak Park Station as part of an ongoing investigations into the four-year-old's disappearance.

image.altText: Gus Lamont

items.title: Public told to stay inside as authorities fight Queensland tyre shop blaze

children.content: Emergency services were called to the business on Queens Road in Kingston just after 1pm, with more than a dozen fire department crews on the scene.

image.altText: fdb7_AERIALS_KingstonShopFire_2705

items.title: Original track designer steers clear of estimated $100m MotoGP remodel

children.content: Adelaide Street Circuit designer Bob Barnard estimates it could cost more than $100 million to remodel the track for the MotoGP, but the premier says that number has not been mentioned to him.

image.altText: A narrow bitumen road runs between trees and large patches of grass

items.title: Australian students record worst tech literacy results on record

children.content: Australian students have recorded their worst-ever results in national tests that measure digital literacy, with just 37 per cent of year 10 students and 50 per cent of year 6 students assessed as proficient.

image.altText: A pair of unidentifiable hands type on a laptop, with other people on laptops in the background.

items.title: Man accused of making bombs in home laboratory faces extra charges

children.content: A man has been charged with more than a dozen further explosives offences related to an alleged bomb-making lab at his home in a small NSW village.

image.altText: A blue house with two old vehicles parked outside, and emergency vehicle son the street.

items.title: Booming cockroach numbers welcome news on this 'paradise of creatures'

children.content: Researchers say the removal of invasive rodents on Lord Howe Island has sparked a rebound of invertebrate life, including native cockroaches and other insects, vital to the broader ecosystem.

gallery.altText: Native cockroaches in a person's hands.
A young man stands on a grassy hill with two large mountains on an island stretching out behind him.
A mountainous island, taken from the air, on a sun day, with blue skies and clear water.

thumbnail.altText: Native cockroaches in a person's hands.
A young man stands on a grassy hill with two large mountains on an island stretching out behind him.
A mountainous island, taken from the air, on a sun day, with blue skies and clear water.

items.title: Two remaining Whyalla steelworks bidders announced, BlueScope retains last offer right

children.content: Peter Malinauskas has announced M Resources and Jindal Steel are the two bidders remaining for the financially stricken Whyalla steelworks, while BlueScope Steel retains its right of last offer.

image.altText: A large smelter or factory with dust and smoke around

items.title: Preferred bidder announced for troubled Liberty Bell Bay smelter

children.content: A consortium headed by Adroit Capital has been announced as the preferred bidder for the smelter in northern Tasmania, as federal and state government pledge another $5 million to support workers in the weeks ahead.

image.altText: A large smelter looking dark and deserted.

items.title: Fidel Castro's daughter fled his regime, and has a warning for the US

children.content: Fidel Castro's daughter says the US has failed to find a way to deal with Cuba for decades. But she tells the ABC that the Trump administration has a difference that could spell the end of the regime she fled.

image.altText: A photo of a young woman in a white lace dress, posing with a man in a green military uniform and thick black beard.

items.title: Women and children linked to ISIS spend first night back in Australia

children.content: A group of so-called ISIS brides who left the notorious al-Roj camp in Syria last week have spent their first night home in Australia.

image.altText: A woman in back seat of a car wearing a face mask.

items.title: Concussion saw Macy lose her 'spark', but a new trial helped her recover

children.content: Macy became a shell of her usual self after suffering a concussion on a school trip, but experienced a huge improvement after a trial designed to help children.

gallery.altText: A young woman with long brown hair poses and smiles next to her mother, who has shoulder length brown hair.

thumbnail.altText: A young woman with long brown hair poses and smiles next to her mother, who has shoulder length brown hair.

avatar.alt: Profile picture of ABC journalist Kate O'Halloran. She is smiling, wearing a black polo shirt with a white stripe on the collar

items.title: BP removes chair over 'serious concerns' about conduct

children.content: The BBC reported Albert Manifold's departure followed concerns about "bullying" and "overbearing" behaviour, which he later described as a "false narrative".

image.altText: A man smiling in a suit.

items.title: 'Not far away': Rescuers confident of reaching group trapped in Laos cave

children.content: Seven people remain trapped after heavy rain triggered a landslide but rescuers say they are close to reaching the group.

image.altText: Four men stand at a narrow cave entrance wearing helmets and carrying ropes.

items.title: 'Can't put it into words': Origin star's mum reveals heartbreaking call

children.content: Cameron Munster's mum reveals the heartbreaking call she had to make before her superstar son led Queensland to victory days after the shock death of his dad.

articles.title: 'Kidding ourselves': Daley whacks talk of Cleary's losing Origin record

children.content: NSW coach Laurie Daley says the responsibility does not solely rest on the shoulders of his star No.7 ahead of this year's State of Origin campaign.

image.altText: Nathan Cleary stands with his hands on his hips

articles.title: The NSW Blues made a 'tough' selection call, so will the Wallabies

children.content: Just as NSW Blues coach Laurie Daley had a difficult selection decision to make, Joe Schmidt may be thinking long and hard about one position in the Wallabies' back line for July's Nations Championship Tests.

image.altText: Max Jorgensen runs onto the field for the Waratahs.

gallery.altText: A tearful Queensland coach Billy Slater hugs skipper Cameron Munster after this year's Origin decider.

thumbnail.altText: A tearful Queensland coach Billy Slater hugs skipper Cameron Munster after this year's Origin decider.

items.title: Australian underdog stuns former world number one at French Open

children.content: Queenslander Adam Walton makes it through the heat and five-set grind to down former world number one Daniil Medvedev at the French Open.

image.altText: Adam Walton, wearing a white shirt, holds his hands in the air and smiles.

items.title: Scott's sacking gives Essendon another chance to step out of Hird shadow

children.content: Essendon finds itself at a clear demarcation point after sacking Brad Scott as the shadow of James Hird once again looms large over the troubled club.

image.altText: James Hird scratches his chin with his right hand
